title: spartium scoparium var. andreanum
intro: Spartium scoparium var. andreanum, also known as Andrachne scoparia, is a variant of the French lavender or tree broom plant, a flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Andrachne scoparia can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
